reasoning: Multiple strong evidence points converge on very low bankruptcy probability. Key findings: (1) Chegg had $528M cash/investments and $42M net cash position as of Feb 2025 earnings (search_0_summary) - this is a solvent company with substantial liquidity. (2) Management targets $100M+ free cash flow for 2025. (3) Stock is down 84% with sub-$200M market cap despite strong balance sheet, suggesting market pricing in business model collapse but not imminent bankruptcy. (4) March 15, 2025 bond maturity had 10.32% distress yield, but no news of default suggests successful payment/refinancing. (5) Company has been proactively restructuring (21% headcount reduction, $40-50M savings targeted). With 2 months to resolution date and strong cash position, bankruptcy probability is extremely low. The only risk is catastrophic business collapse or hidden contingent liabilities, but no evidence supports this. Base rate for public companies with $500M+ cash and positive FCF target filing bankruptcy within 2 months is near zero.
